questions.question: Which ancient Indian university was known for its emphasis on Buddhist studies and was located in present-day Bihar?
questions.explanation: Nalanda University, located in present-day Bihar, was one of the ancient world's most renowned Buddhist universities.

questions.question: During the Mughal era, which emperor is credited with constructing the Taj Mahal?
questions.explanation: Shah Jahan commissioned the Taj Mahal in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal during his reign.

questions.question: Who was the leader of the Non-Cooperation Movement in India?
questions.explanation: Mahatma Gandhi led the Non-Cooperation Movement in 1920 to protest against British rule.

questions.question: The Indian National Army (INA) was led by which prominent leader during the Indian independence movement?
questions.explanation: Subhas Chandra Bose was the leader of the INA, which aimed to overthrow British rule with Japanese support.

questions.question: Which event marked the beginning of India's modern history?
options: The Establishment of British East India Company
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey in 1757 marked the beginning of British dominance in India, initiating modern colonial rule.

questions.question: Which river is the longest in India?
questions.explanation: The Godavari is the longest river in India, flowing approximately 1,465 km.

questions.question: Which mountain range forms the northern boundary of India?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas form the northern boundary of India, acting as a natural border with Tibet and China.

questions.question: Which resource is most abundantly found in the state of Jharkhand?
questions.explanation: Jharkhand is rich in coal deposits, making it a major coal-producing state.

questions.question: The monsoon season in India is primarily caused by which of the following?
questions.explanation: Southwest Monsoon Winds bring heavy rainfall to India during the summer months.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Justice of India as of 2025?
questions.explanation: Surya Kant is the current Chief Justice of India since 24 November 2025.

questions.question: The 'Directive Principles of State Policy' are enshrined in which part of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: Part IV of the Indian Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy.

questions.question: Who has the authority to dissolve the Lok Sabha?
questions.explanation: The President of India has the power to dissolve the Lok Sabha as per constitutional provisions.

questions.question: Which of the following is a Fundamental Right relating to the freedom of speech and expression?
questions.explanation: The Right to Freedom guarantees the freedom of speech and expression.

questions.question: The Union Budget of India is presented by the:
questions.explanation: The Finance Minister presents the Union Budget in Parliament annually.

questions.question: India's GDP growth rate is primarily measured by which national agency?
options: Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation regularly publishes India's GDP growth figures.

questions.question: Which sector contributes the largest share to India’s GDP as of 2025?
questions.explanation: The services sector contributes the largest share to India's GDP, exceeding agriculture and industry.

questions.question: The 'Make in India' initiative primarily aims to promote:
questions.explanation: 'Make in India' promotes manufacturing and aims to make India a global manufacturing hub.

questions.question: Which particle has the highest charge in the atom?
questions.explanation: Protons carry a positive charge and have the highest charge among subatomic particles within an atom.

questions.question: The chemical formula of common salt is:
questions.explanation: NaCl is the chemical formula for sodium chloride, common table salt.

questions.question: Which part of the human body is primarily responsible for the production of insulin?
questions.explanation: The pancreas produces insulin, which regulates blood sugar levels.

questions.question: The Hubble Space Telescope is most closely associated with which field?
questions.explanation: Hubble Space Telescope is used for astronomical observations and studying celestial objects.

questions.question: Which is the world's largest biodiversity hotspot located in India?
questions.explanation: The Western Ghats are recognized as a major biodiversity hotspot in India.

questions.question: What is the primary cause of climate change globally?
questions.explanation: Industrialization leads to increased greenhouse gases, which are the main cause of climate change.

questions.question: Which international treaty aims to protect endangered species and their habitats?
questions.explanation: CITES (Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species) aims to protect endangered species.

questions.question: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) was established primarily to deal with issues related to:
questions.explanation: The NGT is a specialized tribunal for environmental protection and conservation of forests and other natural resources.

questions.question: Who is the current President of India as of 2025?
questions.explanation: Droupadi Murmu has been serving as the President of India since 25 July 2022.

questions.question: Which country is the host of the 2026 FIFA World Cup?
questions.explanation: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will be jointly hosted by the United States, Canada, and Mexico.

questions.question: The 'Startup India' scheme was launched in which year?
questions.explanation: Startup India was launched in 2016 to promote entrepreneurship and innovation.

questions.question: Who was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2024?
questions.explanation: The World Food Programme was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize in 2024 for its efforts to combat hunger.

questions.question: Which Indian sports person was conferred the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna award in 2024?
questions.explanation: Neeraj Chopra received the Rajiv Gandhi Khel Ratna award in 2024 for his achievements in athletics.
